records.ety: [AS. wælisc, welisc, from wealh a stranger, foreigner, not of Saxon origin, a Welshman, a Celt, Gael; akin to OHG. walh, whence G. wälsch or welsch, Celtic, Welsh, Italian, French, Foreign, strange, OHG. walhisc; from the name of a Celtic tribe. See Walnut.]

defs: Of or pertaining to Wales, or its inhabitants.
The language of Wales, or of the Welsh people.
The natives or inhabitants of Wales.
notes: ☞ The Welsh call themselves Cymry, in the plural, and a Welshman Cymro, and their country Cymru, of which the adjective is Cymreig, and the name of their language Cymraeg. They are a branch of the Celtic family, and a relic of the earliest known population of England, driven into the mountains of Wales by the Anglo-Saxon invaders.

defs: To cheat by avoiding payment of bets; -- said esp. of an absconding bookmaker at a race track.
To avoid dishonorably the fulfillment of a pecuniary obligation.
